sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Radio and radar systems are essential for modern aviation navigation and control.

sub_modules.title: Evolution of Navigation
sub_modules.content: Navigation evolved from visual and astro-navigation to radio and satellite systems.

sub_modules.title: Modern Navigation Systems
sub_modules.content: GNSS and radar enable global navigation and air traffic control.

sub_modules.title: Radio Navigation Syllabus
sub_modules.content: Covers radio wave behavior, transmission, reception and signal processing.

sub_modules.title: Electromagnetic Radiation
sub_modules.content: Alternating current produces electromagnetic waves consisting of electric and magnetic fields.

sub_modules.title: EM Wave Components
sub_modules.content: Electric field is parallel and magnetic field is perpendicular to the conductor.

sub_modules.title: Polarization
sub_modules.content: Defined by the orientation of the electric field relative to the antenna.

sub_modules.title: Vertical Polarization
sub_modules.content: Produced by vertical antennas with electric field in vertical plane.

sub_modules.title: Horizontal Polarization
sub_modules.content: Produced by horizontal antennas with electric field in horizontal plane.

sub_modules.title: Circular Polarization
sub_modules.content: Electric field rotates, useful in radar and satellite systems.

sub_modules.title: Polarization Matching
sub_modules.content: Receiver antenna must align with wave polarization for maximum signal strength.

sub_modules.title: Radio Wave Period
sub_modules.content: Time for one cycle of a wave, measured in microseconds.

sub_modules.title: Frequency
sub_modules.content: Number of cycles per second measured in Hertz.

sub_modules.title: Frequency Units
sub_modules.content: Includes kHz, MHz and GHz for practical measurement.

sub_modules.title: Wavelength
sub_modules.content: Distance travelled in one cycle of a wave.

sub_modules.title: Speed of Radio Waves
sub_modules.content: Radio waves travel at the speed of light (3 × 10^8 m/s).

sub_modules.title: Frequency-Wavelength Relationship
sub_modules.content: Frequency and wavelength are inversely proportional.

sub_modules.title: Calculation Formula
sub_modules.content: λ = c / f and f = c / λ.

sub_modules.title: Frequency Bands
sub_modules.content: Radio spectrum divided into bands from VLF to EHF.

sub_modules.title: VLF Band
sub_modules.content: 3–30 kHz, very long wavelengths, limited aviation use.

sub_modules.title: LF Band
sub_modules.content: 30–300 kHz, used for NDB/ADF navigation.

sub_modules.title: MF Band
sub_modules.content: 300–3000 kHz, used for NDB and communications.

sub_modules.title: HF Band
sub_modules.content: 3–30 MHz, used for long-range communication.

sub_modules.title: VHF Band
sub_modules.content: 30–300 MHz, used for VOR, ILS and communication.

sub_modules.title: UHF Band
sub_modules.content: 300–3000 MHz, used for DME, radar and GNSS.

sub_modules.title: SHF Band
sub_modules.content: 3–30 GHz, used for radar and radio altimeter.

sub_modules.title: EHF Band
sub_modules.content: 30–300 GHz, minimal aviation use.

sub_modules.title: Phase Comparison
sub_modules.content: Used in navigation systems to compare two signals.

sub_modules.title: Reference and Variable Signals
sub_modules.content: One signal is reference and the other is compared.

sub_modules.title: Phase Difference
sub_modules.content: Measured in degrees and must always be positive.

sub_modules.title: Phase Calculation
sub_modules.content: If negative, add 360 degrees to obtain correct value.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Radio propagation describes how radio waves travel through the atmosphere.

sub_modules.title: Propagation Importance
sub_modules.content: Different frequencies follow different paths affecting communication and navigation.

sub_modules.title: Attenuation
sub_modules.content: Loss of signal strength as radio waves travel away from transmitter.

sub_modules.title: Absorption
sub_modules.content: Energy loss due to interaction with air, water vapor, terrain and particles.

sub_modules.title: Inverse Square Law
sub_modules.content: Signal strength decreases proportional to the square of distance.

sub_modules.title: Range vs Power
sub_modules.content: Doubling range requires four times the transmitter power.

sub_modules.title: Static Interference
sub_modules.content: Electrical noise reduces signal clarity, especially at low frequencies.

sub_modules.title: Signal to Noise Ratio
sub_modules.content: Quality depends on strength of signal relative to noise.

sub_modules.title: Fading
sub_modules.content: Signal variation caused by multiple paths and phase differences.

sub_modules.title: Power Influence
sub_modules.content: Higher transmitter power increases signal range.

sub_modules.title: Receiver Sensitivity
sub_modules.content: Better receivers detect weaker signals, improving range.

sub_modules.title: Directivity
sub_modules.content: Focused transmission increases range in a specific direction.

sub_modules.title: Propagation Paths
sub_modules.content: Includes surface wave, space wave, sky wave and satellite propagation.

sub_modules.title: Non-Ionospheric Propagation
sub_modules.content: Includes surface wave and space wave paths.

sub_modules.title: Surface Wave
sub_modules.content: Travels along earth surface using diffraction.

sub_modules.title: Surface Wave Range Factors
sub_modules.content: Depends on frequency, surface type and polarization.

sub_modules.title: Sea vs Land Propagation
sub_modules.content: Better propagation over sea due to higher conductivity.

sub_modules.title: Surface Wave Usage
sub_modules.content: Used in LF and MF bands such as NDB/ADF.

sub_modules.title: Space Wave
sub_modules.content: Line-of-sight propagation including direct and reflected waves.

sub_modules.title: Radio Horizon
sub_modules.content: Maximum range determined by line-of-sight limits.

sub_modules.title: Range Formula
sub_modules.content: Range = 1.23 × (√hTX + √hRX) in nautical miles.

sub_modules.title: Line of Sight Limitation
sub_modules.content: Signal cannot be received beyond horizon regardless of power.

sub_modules.title: Ionospheric Propagation
sub_modules.content: Propagation affected by ionized upper atmosphere layers.

sub_modules.title: Ionosphere
sub_modules.content: Region from 60 km to 1500 km containing ionized particles.

sub_modules.title: Ionization Process
sub_modules.content: Solar radiation creates ions and free electrons.

sub_modules.title: Ionospheric Layers
sub_modules.content: D, E and F layers with varying properties.

sub_modules.title: D Layer
sub_modules.content: Lowest layer, absorbs signals and disappears at night.

sub_modules.title: E Layer
sub_modules.content: Middle layer responsible for some signal refraction.

sub_modules.title: F Layer
sub_modules.content: Highest layer, splits into F1 and F2, major role in HF propagation.

sub_modules.title: Sky Wave
sub_modules.content: Radio waves refracted back to earth by ionosphere.

sub_modules.title: Critical Angle
sub_modules.content: Angle at which total internal refraction occurs.

sub_modules.title: Skip Distance
sub_modules.content: Distance from transmitter to first returned sky wave.

sub_modules.title: Dead Space
sub_modules.content: Area with no signal between surface wave and sky wave.

sub_modules.title: Frequency Effect
sub_modules.content: Higher frequency increases skip distance and reduces refraction.

sub_modules.title: Ionization Effect
sub_modules.content: Higher ionization reduces skip distance.

sub_modules.title: Layer Height Effect
sub_modules.content: Higher layers increase propagation range.

sub_modules.title: LF/MF Sky Wave
sub_modules.content: Occurs at night when D-layer disappears.

sub_modules.title: Multi-hop Propagation
sub_modules.content: Signal reflects multiple times between earth and ionosphere.

sub_modules.title: HF Communication
sub_modules.content: Uses sky wave for long-range communication.

sub_modules.title: Maximum Usable Frequency
sub_modules.content: Highest frequency that returns to earth for given conditions.

sub_modules.title: Optimum Working Frequency
sub_modules.content: Typically 85% of MUF for reliable communication.

sub_modules.title: Day vs Night Frequencies
sub_modules.content: Lower frequencies used at night due to reduced ionization.

sub_modules.title: Propagation Summary
sub_modules.content: Different frequency bands use specific propagation paths.

sub_modules.title: Super-refraction
sub_modules.content: Increased bending of waves extending range beyond normal.

sub_modules.title: Duct Propagation
sub_modules.content: Traps waves allowing very long distance transmission.

sub_modules.title: Sub-refraction
sub_modules.content: Reduced bending causing decreased radio range.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Modulation is the process of adding information to a radio wave.

sub_modules.title: Purpose of Modulation
sub_modules.content: Used for transmitting audio, data and navigation information.

sub_modules.title: Types of Modulation
sub_modules.content: Includes keyed, AM, FM, phase and pulse modulation.

sub_modules.title: Audio Signal Conversion
sub_modules.content: Microphones convert sound waves into electrical signals.

sub_modules.title: Keyed Modulation
sub_modules.content: Carrier wave is switched on and off to transmit information.

sub_modules.title: Morse Code
sub_modules.content: Uses short and long signals for communication.

sub_modules.title: NDB Identification
sub_modules.content: Keyed modulation is used for beacon identification.

sub_modules.title: Amplitude Modulation (AM)
sub_modules.content: Audio signal changes the amplitude of the carrier wave.

sub_modules.title: AM Principle
sub_modules.content: Positive audio increases amplitude, negative decreases amplitude.

sub_modules.title: Heterodyning
sub_modules.content: Combining RF and AF produces sum and difference frequencies.

sub_modules.title: Sidebands
sub_modules.content: Upper and lower sidebands carry the transmitted information.

sub_modules.title: Bandwidth in AM
sub_modules.content: Bandwidth equals twice the audio frequency.

sub_modules.title: Power Distribution
sub_modules.content: Only one third of AM signal carries useful information.

sub_modules.title: Single Sideband (SSB)
sub_modules.content: Removes carrier and one sideband to improve efficiency.

sub_modules.title: SSB Advantages
sub_modules.content: Improves signal quality, reduces power and increases channel capacity.

sub_modules.title: SSB in Aviation
sub_modules.content: Used in HF communications for long-range transmission.

sub_modules.title: Frequency Modulation (FM)
sub_modules.content: Audio signal changes the frequency of the carrier wave.

sub_modules.title: FM Characteristics
sub_modules.content: Amplitude remains constant while frequency varies.

sub_modules.title: Frequency Deviation
sub_modules.content: Greater audio amplitude produces greater frequency change.

sub_modules.title: FM Bandwidth
sub_modules.content: Requires larger bandwidth compared to AM.

sub_modules.title: Narrow Band FM
sub_modules.content: Reduced bandwidth FM not widely used in aviation.

sub_modules.title: Phase Modulation
sub_modules.content: Phase of carrier wave is varied according to input signal.

sub_modules.title: Analogue Phase Modulation
sub_modules.content: Phase varies based on amplitude of input signal.

sub_modules.title: Digital Phase Modulation
sub_modules.content: Phase shift keying represents binary data.

sub_modules.title: Phase Modulation in Navigation
sub_modules.content: Used in systems like GPS and MLS.

sub_modules.title: Pulse Modulation
sub_modules.content: Information transmitted using pulses of radio waves.

sub_modules.title: Radar Applications
sub_modules.content: Pulse modulation widely used in radar systems.

sub_modules.title: Emission Designators
sub_modules.content: Standard codes used to describe signal characteristics.

sub_modules.title: Designator Structure
sub_modules.content: Three symbols define modulation type, signal type and information.

sub_modules.title: Example A3E
sub_modules.content: AM double sideband used for voice communication.

sub_modules.title: Example J3E
sub_modules.content: SSB suppressed carrier used in HF communication.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Designators
sub_modules.content: Different systems like VOR, ILS and DME use specific designations.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Antennae radiate and receive radio energy for communication and navigation systems.

sub_modules.title: Antenna Function
sub_modules.content: Type of antenna depends on system requirements and frequency.

sub_modules.title: Basic Antenna Types
sub_modules.content: Includes half-wave dipole and quarter-wave Marconi antennas.

sub_modules.title: Half-Wave Dipole
sub_modules.content: Radiates energy perpendicular to the antenna with feed at the center.

sub_modules.title: Marconi Antenna
sub_modules.content: Quarter-wave antenna using aircraft body as ground plane.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Antennas
sub_modules.content: Marconi antennas preferred due to aerodynamic advantages.

sub_modules.title: Antenna Length
sub_modules.content: Must match wavelength (half or quarter wavelength) for efficiency.

sub_modules.title: Medium Effect
sub_modules.content: Wave speed changes in different media affecting antenna length.

sub_modules.title: Wavelength Formula
sub_modules.content: λ = 300 / f (MHz) used for antenna calculations.

sub_modules.title: Aerial Feeders
sub_modules.content: Transmit energy between antenna and equipment.

sub_modules.title: Low Frequency Feeders
sub_modules.content: Simple wires used for LF and MF with minimal losses.

sub_modules.title: HF and VHF Feeders
sub_modules.content: Twin wire feeders used to reduce losses.

sub_modules.title: UHF Feeders
sub_modules.content: Coaxial cables required due to higher losses.

sub_modules.title: Waveguides
sub_modules.content: Used at very high frequencies to carry energy efficiently.

sub_modules.title: Polar Diagrams
sub_modules.content: Graphical representation of radiation pattern.

sub_modules.title: Omnidirectional Pattern
sub_modules.content: Dipole radiates equally in all directions perpendicular to antenna.

sub_modules.title: 3D Radiation Pattern
sub_modules.content: Radiation forms a torus shape around antenna.

sub_modules.title: Directivity
sub_modules.content: Ability to focus energy in a specific direction.

sub_modules.title: Reflector Element
sub_modules.content: Parasitic element reflects energy to increase forward signal.

sub_modules.title: Directors
sub_modules.content: Additional elements focus and strengthen signal.

sub_modules.title: Yagi Antenna
sub_modules.content: Combination of reflector and directors for directional gain.

sub_modules.title: Side Lobes
sub_modules.content: Unwanted radiation causing interference and signal distortion.

sub_modules.title: ILS Antenna
sub_modules.content: Array antennas produce narrow beams for runway alignment.

sub_modules.title: Loop Antenna
sub_modules.content: Used in ADF for direction finding.

sub_modules.title: Loop Polar Pattern
sub_modules.content: Figure-of-eight pattern with nulls for direction detection.

sub_modules.title: ADF Principle
sub_modules.content: Signal nulls used to determine bearing.

sub_modules.title: Radar Antenna
sub_modules.content: Uses parabolic reflector to produce narrow beam.

sub_modules.title: Parabolic Reflector
sub_modules.content: Converts point source into parallel wavefront.

sub_modules.title: Radar Beam
sub_modules.content: Produces focused directional signal with some divergence.

sub_modules.title: Radar Side Lobes
sub_modules.content: Unwanted energy causing false targets.

sub_modules.title: Flat Plate Antenna
sub_modules.content: Modern radar antenna with multiple slots.

sub_modules.title: Phased Array
sub_modules.content: Combines multiple signals to form precise beam.

sub_modules.title: Phased Array Advantages
sub_modules.content: Narrow beam, reduced side lobes and improved efficiency.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Doppler systems measure aircraft ground speed and drift using frequency shift.

sub_modules.title: Doppler Applications
sub_modules.content: Used in navigation, radar, VOR and speed measurement systems.

sub_modules.title: Modern Systems
sub_modules.content: Combined with IRS, GPS and VOR/DME for improved accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Doppler Principle
sub_modules.content: Frequency changes due to relative motion between transmitter and receiver.

sub_modules.title: No Relative Motion
sub_modules.content: Received frequency equals transmitted frequency.

sub_modules.title: Approaching Motion
sub_modules.content: Frequency increases when moving towards the source.

sub_modules.title: Receding Motion
sub_modules.content: Frequency decreases when moving away from the source.

sub_modules.title: Doppler Shift
sub_modules.content: Difference between transmitted and received frequency.

sub_modules.title: Doppler Proportionality
sub_modules.content: Frequency shift is proportional to relative speed.

sub_modules.title: Airborne Doppler System
sub_modules.content: Uses radar beams directed towards the ground.

sub_modules.title: Shared Antenna
sub_modules.content: Single antenna used for both transmission and reception.

sub_modules.title: Janus Array
sub_modules.content: Multi-beam configuration with forward and aft beams.

sub_modules.title: Four Beam System
sub_modules.content: Two beams forward and two aft for accurate measurements.

sub_modules.title: Beam Configuration
sub_modules.content: Different beam arrangements used for navigation accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Ground Speed Measurement
sub_modules.content: Based on frequency shift magnitude from forward and aft beams.

sub_modules.title: Zero Drift Condition
sub_modules.content: Forward and aft frequency shifts are equal and opposite.

sub_modules.title: Drift Detection
sub_modules.content: Difference between left and right beam frequencies indicates drift.

sub_modules.title: Drift Direction
sub_modules.content: Frequency imbalance shows left or right drift.

sub_modules.title: Electronic Processing
sub_modules.content: Modern systems calculate drift and speed automatically.

sub_modules.title: Mechanical Systems
sub_modules.content: Older systems used rotating antennas to align with track.

sub_modules.title: Track Alignment
sub_modules.content: Antenna adjusts until port and starboard signals equalize.

sub_modules.title: Heading vs Track
sub_modules.content: Difference between aircraft heading and track gives drift.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Integration
sub_modules.content: Doppler data combined with heading for position calculation.

sub_modules.title: Position Calculation
sub_modules.content: Uses ground speed, drift and heading to determine location.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Output
sub_modules.content: Displays position as latitude/longitude or track distances.

sub_modules.title: Advantages
sub_modules.content: Provides continuous speed and drift information.

sub_modules.title: Limitations
sub_modules.content: Accuracy affected over water and without reference inputs.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: VDF provides pilots with bearing information to a ground station.

sub_modules.title: Purpose of VDF
sub_modules.content: Used for homing or fixing position using bearings.

sub_modules.title: Usage
sub_modules.content: Rarely used due to more advanced navigation systems.

sub_modules.title: Frequency Range
sub_modules.content: Operates on VHF communication frequencies 118.0 to 137 MHz.

sub_modules.title: Distress Frequency
sub_modules.content: Auto-triangulation available on 121.5 MHz.

sub_modules.title: Military Use
sub_modules.content: UHF direction finding mainly used by military.

sub_modules.title: Availability
sub_modules.content: VDF stations listed in AIP and may have usage limitations.

sub_modules.title: Service Conditions
sub_modules.content: Bearings provided only when conditions are satisfactory.

sub_modules.title: Pilot Request
sub_modules.content: Pilot requests bearing using Q-codes like QDM.

sub_modules.title: QDR
sub_modules.content: Magnetic bearing from station (radial).

sub_modules.title: QDM
sub_modules.content: Magnetic heading to fly to reach station.

sub_modules.title: QTE
sub_modules.content: True bearing from the station.

sub_modules.title: QUJ
sub_modules.content: True track to the station.

sub_modules.title: Reciprocal Bearings
sub_modules.content: QDM is reciprocal of QDR, QUJ is reciprocal of QTE.

sub_modules.title: Response Format
sub_modules.content: Includes bearing, class of accuracy and time if required.

sub_modules.title: Accuracy Classes
sub_modules.content: Class A ±2°, Class B ±5°, Class C ±10°, Class D less accurate.

sub_modules.title: Typical Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Most bearings are Class B accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Doppler VDF
sub_modules.content: Modern systems achieve accuracy up to ±0.5°.

sub_modules.title: Principle of Operation
sub_modules.content: Uses VHF transmission received by ground antenna array.

sub_modules.title: Polarization
sub_modules.content: Uses vertically polarized signals.

sub_modules.title: Antenna Array
sub_modules.content: Circular array of vertical antennas determines direction.

sub_modules.title: Bearing Calculation
sub_modules.content: System processes signals from multiple elements to determine bearing.

sub_modules.title: Display
sub_modules.content: Bearing shown relative to true or magnetic north.

sub_modules.title: Range
sub_modules.content: Limited to line-of-sight propagation.

sub_modules.title: Range Factors
sub_modules.content: Affected by altitude, terrain and transmitter power.

sub_modules.title: Extended Range
sub_modules.content: Atmospheric conditions may increase range beyond line of sight.

sub_modules.title: Propagation Error
sub_modules.content: Reflections from terrain and buildings cause inaccuracies.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Attitude
sub_modules.content: Best accuracy when aircraft is level due to polarization.

sub_modules.title: Overhead Error
sub_modules.content: Accuracy reduces when aircraft is directly above station.

sub_modules.title: Multipath Effect
sub_modules.content: Signal reflections cause fading and bearing errors.

sub_modules.title: Interference
sub_modules.content: Simultaneous transmissions can cause errors.

sub_modules.title: Position Fixing
sub_modules.content: Multiple bearings used to determine aircraft position.

sub_modules.title: Auto-Triangulation
sub_modules.content: System automatically calculates position using multiple stations.

sub_modules.title: Limitations
sub_modules.content: Availability and accuracy depend on station coverage and conditions.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: ADF works with NDB to provide bearing information for navigation and approaches.

sub_modules.title: ADF Usage
sub_modules.content: Used for homing, en-route navigation and non-precision approaches.

sub_modules.title: Non-Directional Beacon (NDB)
sub_modules.content: Ground transmitter radiating signals in all directions in LF/MF bands.

sub_modules.title: ADF Function
sub_modules.content: Indicates direction of NDB relative to aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Cone of Silence
sub_modules.content: No signal received when directly overhead the NDB.

sub_modules.title: Principle of Operation
sub_modules.content: Uses loop antenna to detect signal direction based on phase difference.

sub_modules.title: Loop Antenna
sub_modules.content: Produces figure-of-eight pattern with two null points.

sub_modules.title: Ambiguity Problem
sub_modules.content: Two nulls create uncertainty in direction.

sub_modules.title: Sense Antenna
sub_modules.content: Omnidirectional antenna removes ambiguity.

sub_modules.title: Cardioid Pattern
sub_modules.content: Combination of loop and sense antenna produces single null.

sub_modules.title: Switched Cardioid
sub_modules.content: Rapid switching improves bearing accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Goniometer
sub_modules.content: Simulates rotating loop electrically in modern systems.

sub_modules.title: ADF Frequencies
sub_modules.content: Operates between 190 and 1750 kHz (LF/MF bands).

sub_modules.title: Types of NDB
sub_modules.content: Locator (short range) and en-route (long range) beacons.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Equipment
sub_modules.content: Includes loop antenna, sense antenna, receiver, control unit and display.

sub_modules.title: Emission Types
sub_modules.content: N0NA1A and N0NA2A transmissions.

sub_modules.title: BFO
sub_modules.content: Used to detect unmodulated signals for identification.

sub_modules.title: ADF Displays
sub_modules.content: Information shown on RBI or RMI.

sub_modules.title: Relative Bearing
sub_modules.content: Angle between aircraft heading and beacon direction.

sub_modules.title: RBI
sub_modules.content: Fixed compass card showing relative bearing only.

sub_modules.title: RMI
sub_modules.content: Rotating compass card showing magnetic bearing.

sub_modules.title: QDM and QDR
sub_modules.content: Needle head gives QDM, tail gives QDR.

sub_modules.title: Uses of ADF
sub_modules.content: Includes homing, tracking, holding and instrument approaches.

sub_modules.title: Homing
sub_modules.content: Aircraft follows needle directly to beacon.

sub_modules.title: Tracking
sub_modules.content: Maintains desired track with drift correction.

sub_modules.title: Inbound Tracking
sub_modules.content: Heading adjusted to maintain constant track to beacon.

sub_modules.title: Outbound Tracking
sub_modules.content: Heading adjusted to maintain track away from beacon.

sub_modules.title: Drift Correction
sub_modules.content: Wind effect compensated by adjusting heading.

sub_modules.title: Holding
sub_modules.content: Aircraft flies racetrack pattern over beacon.

sub_modules.title: Instrument Approaches
sub_modules.content: Used for non-precision runway approaches.

sub_modules.title: ADF Accuracy
sub_modules.content: ±5° during daytime within coverage area.

sub_modules.title: Static Interference
sub_modules.content: Caused by precipitation and thunderstorms.

sub_modules.title: Night Effect
sub_modules.content: Sky wave interference affects accuracy at night.

sub_modules.title: Station Interference
sub_modules.content: Signals from other stations cause errors.

sub_modules.title: Mountain Effect
sub_modules.content: Terrain reflections distort signals.

sub_modules.title: Coastal Refraction
sub_modules.content: Signal bends when crossing land-sea boundary.

sub_modules.title: Quadrantal Error
sub_modules.content: Aircraft structure distorts signal reception.

sub_modules.title: Dip Error
sub_modules.content: Bank angle causes bearing inaccuracies.

sub_modules.title: Failure Limitations
sub_modules.content: Lack of warning system requires constant monitoring.

sub_modules.title: Range Factors
sub_modules.content: Depends on power, frequency, terrain and weather.

sub_modules.title: ADF Summary
sub_modules.content: Provides bearing using NDB but affected by multiple errors.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: VOR is a short-range navigation aid that provides magnetic bearing (radial) from the station.

sub_modules.title: Frequency Range
sub_modules.content: Operates in the VHF band between 108.0 and 117.95 MHz.

sub_modules.title: Uses of VOR
sub_modules.content: Used for airway navigation, holding, approaches, and position fixing.

sub_modules.title: Rho-Theta Navigation
sub_modules.content: When combined with DME, VOR provides both bearing (theta) and distance (rho).

sub_modules.title: Principle of Operation
sub_modules.content: Based on phase comparison between two 30 Hz signals.

sub_modules.title: Reference Signal
sub_modules.content: Omni-directional FM signal with constant phase.

sub_modules.title: Variable Signal
sub_modules.content: Directional AM signal with rotating phase (limaçon pattern).

sub_modules.title: Phase Difference
sub_modules.content: Measured phase difference corresponds to magnetic bearing from the station.

sub_modules.title: Radial
sub_modules.content: A radial is the magnetic bearing FROM the VOR (QDR).

sub_modules.title: Transmission Characteristics
sub_modules.content: Composite AM signal (A9W) including navigation and voice/ATIS.

sub_modules.title: Identification
sub_modules.content: 3-letter Morse code or voice ident transmitted regularly.

sub_modules.title: Monitoring
sub_modules.content: Automatic monitoring ensures accuracy within ±1° and removes faulty signals.

sub_modules.title: Types of VOR
sub_modules.content: Includes CVOR, DVOR, TVOR, BVOR, VOT and VORTAC.

sub_modules.title: Doppler VOR (DVOR)
sub_modules.content: Improves accuracy by reducing site error using Doppler principle.

sub_modules.title: Operational Range
sub_modules.content: Depends on transmitter power and line-of-sight propagation.

sub_modules.title: Range Formula
sub_modules.content: Maximum range = 1.23 × (√h1 + √h2) where heights are in feet.

sub_modules.title: Designated Operational Coverage (DOC)
sub_modules.content: Defines protected range and altitude to avoid interference.

sub_modules.title: Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Total system accuracy is approximately ±5°.

sub_modules.title: Errors
sub_modules.content: Includes site error, propagation error (scalloping), and airborne equipment error.

sub_modules.title: Cone of Confusion
sub_modules.content: Unstable indications when directly overhead the VOR.

sub_modules.title: Airborne Equipment
sub_modules.content: Consists of antenna, receiver and display system.

sub_modules.title: CDI (Course Deviation Indicator)
sub_modules.content: Displays deviation from selected radial using deviation bar.

sub_modules.title: OBS (Omni Bearing Selector)
sub_modules.content: Used to select desired course or radial.

sub_modules.title: Deviation Indication
sub_modules.content: 1 dot equals 2° deviation; full scale deflection equals 10°.

sub_modules.title: TO/FROM Indicator
sub_modules.content: Indicates whether selected course leads to or from the station.

sub_modules.title: CDI Rule
sub_modules.content: Select inbound course with TO indication and outbound course with FROM indication.

sub_modules.title: RMI (Radio Magnetic Indicator)
sub_modules.content: Displays bearing directly to station.

sub_modules.title: RMI Indications
sub_modules.content: Arrowhead shows QDM (to station), tail shows QDR (radial).

sub_modules.title: HSI (Horizontal Situation Indicator)
sub_modules.content: Combines heading and CDI for intuitive navigation display.

sub_modules.title: Radial Interception
sub_modules.content: Achieved by intercept angle and drift correction.

sub_modules.title: Track Keeping
sub_modules.content: Maintaining selected radial using CDI corrections.

sub_modules.title: Station Passage
sub_modules.content: Needle fluctuations and TO/FROM reversal occur overhead.

sub_modules.title: Outbound Tracking
sub_modules.content: Maintaining radial away from station.

sub_modules.title: Approach Procedures
sub_modules.content: Used in VOR and VOR/DME instrument approaches.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: VOR provides reliable magnetic bearing information, largely unaffected by weather.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: ILS is a precision approach system providing both lateral and vertical guidance for landing.

sub_modules.title: Purpose of ILS
sub_modules.content: Guides aircraft along runway centreline and glide path to decision height.

sub_modules.title: Main Components
sub_modules.content: Localizer, glide path and marker beacons form the ground system.

sub_modules.title: Localizer (LLZ)
sub_modules.content: Provides lateral guidance along runway centreline using VHF signals.

sub_modules.title: Glide Path (GP)
sub_modules.content: Provides vertical guidance along a fixed descent angle using UHF signals.

sub_modules.title: Marker Beacons
sub_modules.content: Provide distance checkpoints using 75 MHz signals (OM, MM, IM).

sub_modules.title: Back Course
sub_modules.content: Reverse localizer signal used for non-precision approaches in some regions.

sub_modules.title: Locator and DME
sub_modules.content: Locator NDB or DME may supplement or replace marker beacons.

sub_modules.title: Localizer Frequency
sub_modules.content: Operates between 108 and 111.975 MHz.

sub_modules.title: Glide Path Frequency
sub_modules.content: Operates between 329.15 and 335 MHz.

sub_modules.title: Frequency Pairing
sub_modules.content: Localizer and glide path frequencies are paired for automatic selection.

sub_modules.title: ILS Identification
sub_modules.content: 2 or 3 letter Morse code, usually starting with 'I'.

sub_modules.title: Marker Beacon Identification
sub_modules.content: OM (blue), MM (amber), IM (white) with distinct tones and patterns.

sub_modules.title: Ground Monitoring
sub_modules.content: System automatically monitors signal accuracy and shuts down if limits exceeded.

sub_modules.title: Localizer Coverage
sub_modules.content: Up to 25 NM within ±10° and reduced coverage beyond.

sub_modules.title: Glide Path Coverage
sub_modules.content: Up to 10 NM with vertical coverage between 0.45θ and 1.75θ.

sub_modules.title: Localizer Principle
sub_modules.content: Uses 90 Hz and 150 Hz modulated overlapping lobes for lateral guidance.

sub_modules.title: DDM (Difference in Depth of Modulation)
sub_modules.content: Difference between 90 Hz and 150 Hz signals determines deviation.

sub_modules.title: Localizer Indication
sub_modules.content: Balanced signals indicate centreline; imbalance gives left/right correction.

sub_modules.title: Glide Path Principle
sub_modules.content: Uses vertical lobes with 90 Hz (above) and 150 Hz (below).

sub_modules.title: Glide Path Indication
sub_modules.content: Balanced signals indicate correct descent path.

sub_modules.title: False Glide Slope
sub_modules.content: False signals occur above true glide path due to reflections.

sub_modules.title: Cone of Confusion
sub_modules.content: Unstable signals near runway threshold or overhead transmitters.

sub_modules.title: ILS Reference Datum
sub_modules.content: Point where glide path crosses runway threshold (~50 ft height).

sub_modules.title: Visual Glide Aids
sub_modules.content: PAPI/VASI provide visual confirmation of glide path.

sub_modules.title: ILS Displays
sub_modules.content: Displayed on CDI or HSI with lateral and vertical needles.

sub_modules.title: Localizer Sensitivity
sub_modules.content: 0.5° per dot, full scale 2.5°.

sub_modules.title: Glide Path Sensitivity
sub_modules.content: 0.14° per dot, full scale 0.7°.

sub_modules.title: Approach Limits
sub_modules.content: Exceeding half-scale deflection requires go-around.

sub_modules.title: ILS Categories
sub_modules.content: Cat I, II, III define landing capability based on DH and RVR.

sub_modules.title: Category I
sub_modules.content: DH ≥ 200 ft, RVR ≥ 550 m.

sub_modules.title: Category II
sub_modules.content: DH ≥ 100 ft, RVR ≥ 300 m.

sub_modules.title: Category III
sub_modules.content: Very low or zero DH with reduced RVR requirements.

sub_modules.title: Errors
sub_modules.content: Includes beam bends, scalloping and signal noise.

sub_modules.title: Multipath Interference
sub_modules.content: Reflections from terrain or objects distort signals.

sub_modules.title: Critical Area
sub_modules.content: Protected area near antennas to avoid interference.

sub_modules.title: Sensitive Area
sub_modules.content: Extended protection area during low visibility operations.

sub_modules.title: Weather Effects
sub_modules.content: Rain and snow reduce signal strength and accuracy.

sub_modules.title: FM Interference
sub_modules.content: Nearby FM transmissions may affect ILS signals.

sub_modules.title: Approach Segments
sub_modules.content: Initial, intermediate and final approach phases.

sub_modules.title: Decision Height (DH)
sub_modules.content: Altitude where pilot decides to land or go around.

sub_modules.title: ILS Calculations
sub_modules.content: 1:60 rule used for height and rate of descent estimation.

sub_modules.title: Rate of Descent Rule
sub_modules.content: For 3° glide path, ROD ≈ 5 × ground speed.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: ILS provides precise and reliable landing guidance using radio signals.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: MLS is an advanced precision approach system designed to replace ILS with improved flexibility and performance.

sub_modules.title: Purpose of MLS
sub_modules.content: Provides azimuth, elevation and range information for precise aircraft positioning during approach and landing.

sub_modules.title: ILS Limitations
sub_modules.content: Limited channels, fixed beams, terrain restrictions and susceptibility to interference.

sub_modules.title: MLS Channel Capacity
sub_modules.content: Provides around 200 channels compared to ILS limitations.

sub_modules.title: Coverage
sub_modules.content: Azimuth coverage ±40° and range up to 20–30 NM.

sub_modules.title: Glide Slope Flexibility
sub_modules.content: Selectable glide slopes from 0.9° to 20°.

sub_modules.title: Back Azimuth
sub_modules.content: Provides guidance for go-around and departure.

sub_modules.title: Operating Frequency
sub_modules.content: Operates in SHF band between 5031 and 5090.7 MHz.

sub_modules.title: Terrain Advantage
sub_modules.content: Can be installed in hilly terrain without performance degradation.

sub_modules.title: Interference Resistance
sub_modules.content: Less affected by reflections from buildings, vehicles and terrain.

sub_modules.title: Flexible Approaches
sub_modules.content: Allows curved and segmented approaches improving traffic flow.

sub_modules.title: Built-in DME
sub_modules.content: Provides range information without separate marker beacons.

sub_modules.title: Compatibility
sub_modules.content: Works with existing ILS displays, autopilot and EFIS systems.

sub_modules.title: Automatic Landing Capability
sub_modules.content: Supports CAT III automatic landing operations.

sub_modules.title: Identification
sub_modules.content: Uses prefix 'M' followed by two letters.

sub_modules.title: Principle of Operation
sub_modules.content: Based on Time Division Multiplexing (TDM) using scanning beams.

sub_modules.title: Azimuth Measurement
sub_modules.content: Determined by time difference between 'to' and 'fro' scanning beams.

sub_modules.title: Elevation Measurement
sub_modules.content: Determined by time difference between upward and downward scans.

sub_modules.title: Time Referenced Scanning Beam (TRSB)
sub_modules.content: Scanning beams used to calculate angular position of aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Range Measurement
sub_modules.content: Provided using integrated DME or precision DME (DME/P).

sub_modules.title: Auxiliary Data
sub_modules.content: Includes station identification, system status, weather and runway conditions.

sub_modules.title: Flare Guidance
sub_modules.content: Provision exists but not widely implemented.

sub_modules.title: Back Azimuth Guidance
sub_modules.content: Provides departure and missed approach guidance.

sub_modules.title: Display
sub_modules.content: Uses cross-pointer display similar to ILS but relative to selected path.

sub_modules.title: Curved Approaches
sub_modules.content: Supports non-linear approach paths unlike ILS.

sub_modules.title: Control Unit
sub_modules.content: Multi-mode control unit allows selection of MLS, ILS or GPS.

sub_modules.title: Precision DME (DME/P)
sub_modules.content: Required for high-accuracy CAT II/III approaches.

sub_modules.title: Advantages of MLS
sub_modules.content: Greater flexibility, wider coverage, reduced interference and higher efficiency.

sub_modules.title: Limitations
sub_modules.content: Limited global adoption and coexistence with ILS.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: MLS is a modern precision landing system offering flexible and accurate approach guidance.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Radar (Radio Detection And Ranging) detects objects and measures their distance using radio waves.

sub_modules.title: Basic Principle
sub_modules.content: Radar transmits electromagnetic energy and detects the reflected signal (echo).

sub_modules.title: Continuous Wave Radar
sub_modules.content: Uses continuous signals and has no minimum range limitation.

sub_modules.title: Pulsed Radar
sub_modules.content: Uses short bursts of radio energy to measure distance.

sub_modules.title: Primary Radar
sub_modules.content: Detects targets using reflected radio waves on the same frequency.

sub_modules.title: Secondary Radar
sub_modules.content: Uses interrogation and transponder system with different transmit and receive frequencies.

sub_modules.title: Radar Applications
sub_modules.content: Used in ATC, navigation, weather detection and aircraft tracking.

sub_modules.title: Air Traffic Control Use
sub_modules.content: Monitoring aircraft, separation, vectoring and approach guidance.

sub_modules.title: Weather Radar
sub_modules.content: Detects precipitation, turbulence and cloud structures.

sub_modules.title: Radar Frequencies
sub_modules.content: Operates in VHF and above for better resolution and reduced interference.

sub_modules.title: Pulse Technique
sub_modules.content: Radar transmits pulses separated by time intervals.

sub_modules.title: Pulse Width
sub_modules.content: Duration of a pulse determining minimum range and resolution.

sub_modules.title: Pulse Recurrence Interval (PRI)
sub_modules.content: Time between successive pulses.

sub_modules.title: Pulse Recurrence Frequency (PRF)
sub_modules.content: Number of pulses transmitted per second.

sub_modules.title: Range Measurement
sub_modules.content: Distance calculated using time taken for echo to return.

sub_modules.title: Radar Mile
sub_modules.content: One nautical mile corresponds to 12.36 microseconds round-trip time.

sub_modules.title: Maximum Range
sub_modules.content: Determined by PRF; lower PRF allows longer range.

sub_modules.title: Practical Range
sub_modules.content: Reduced by dead time (fly-back period).

sub_modules.title: Minimum Range
sub_modules.content: Determined by pulse width and receiver recovery time.

sub_modules.title: Echo Principle
sub_modules.content: Radar measures distance by timing transmitted and received signals.

sub_modules.title: Line of Sight
sub_modules.content: Radar range depends on transmitter and target height.

sub_modules.title: Range Formula
sub_modules.content: Range = 1.23 × (√HTX + √HRX).

sub_modules.title: Factors Affecting Range
sub_modules.content: Includes power, target characteristics, height and atmospheric conditions.

sub_modules.title: Atmospheric Effects
sub_modules.content: Super-refraction increases range; sub-refraction reduces range.

sub_modules.title: Rain Attenuation
sub_modules.content: Shorter wavelengths are more affected by rain.

sub_modules.title: Primary Radar Operation
sub_modules.content: Uses single antenna for transmission and reception.

sub_modules.title: PPI Display
sub_modules.content: Plan Position Indicator shows radar returns in polar format.

sub_modules.title: Bearing Measurement
sub_modules.content: Determined by direction of rotating radar beam.

sub_modules.title: Harmonization
sub_modules.content: Synchronization of radar parameters for accurate operation.

sub_modules.title: Radar Resolution
sub_modules.content: Ability to distinguish between two close targets.

sub_modules.title: Radial Resolution
sub_modules.content: Depends on pulse length.

sub_modules.title: Azimuth Resolution
sub_modules.content: Depends on beamwidth.

sub_modules.title: Moving Target Indication (MTI)
sub_modules.content: Filters out stationary objects to show moving targets.

sub_modules.title: Second Trace Returns
sub_modules.content: False echoes caused by previous pulses.

sub_modules.title: PRF Jittering
sub_modules.content: Technique to eliminate false returns.

sub_modules.title: Radar Antennas
sub_modules.content: Includes parabolic reflector, horn and planar array.

sub_modules.title: Beamwidth
sub_modules.content: Narrow beams improve accuracy and resolution.

sub_modules.title: Side Lobes
sub_modules.content: Unwanted radiation reduced using suppression techniques.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: Radar determines range and bearing using reflected radio waves.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: Ground radar is used by ATC for aircraft detection, monitoring and control using primary and secondary radar.

sub_modules.title: Types of Services
sub_modules.content: ATC radar provides information, surveillance and guidance services.

sub_modules.title: Primary Radar Use
sub_modules.content: Detects aircraft without transponders using reflected signals.

sub_modules.title: Secondary Radar Use
sub_modules.content: Provides additional aircraft information using transponders.

sub_modules.title: Ground Radar Types
sub_modules.content: Includes ASR, TAR, Aerodrome Radar, PAR and ASMR.

sub_modules.title: Area Surveillance Radar (ASR)
sub_modules.content: Long-range radar used for en-route airway surveillance.

sub_modules.title: ASR Range
sub_modules.content: Typically covers 200 to 300 nautical miles.

sub_modules.title: ASR Characteristics
sub_modules.content: Uses long wavelengths and pulse widths for better long-range detection.

sub_modules.title: ASR PRF and Scan Rate
sub_modules.content: Low PRF (300–400 pps) and slow antenna rotation (5–6 rpm).

sub_modules.title: Terminal Area Radar (TAR)
sub_modules.content: Medium-range radar used for traffic control in terminal areas.

sub_modules.title: TAR Range
sub_modules.content: Typically up to 75 nautical miles.

sub_modules.title: TAR Characteristics
sub_modules.content: Uses moderate wavelengths and pulse widths.

sub_modules.title: Aerodrome Surveillance Radar
sub_modules.content: Short-range radar used near airports.

sub_modules.title: Aerodrome Radar Range
sub_modules.content: Typically up to 25 nautical miles.

sub_modules.title: Approach Radar Functions
sub_modules.content: Provides vectoring, positional information and surveillance approaches.

sub_modules.title: Precision Approach Radar (PAR)
sub_modules.content: Provides highly accurate guidance for landing.

sub_modules.title: Airport Surface Movement Radar (ASMR)
sub_modules.content: Used for monitoring aircraft and vehicles on the ground.

sub_modules.title: ASMR Purpose
sub_modules.content: Ensures safe movement on runways and taxiways in low visibility.

sub_modules.title: ASMR Range
sub_modules.content: Typically 2.5 to 6 nautical miles.

sub_modules.title: ASMR Beamwidth
sub_modules.content: Very narrow beam (0.2° to 1°) for high resolution.

sub_modules.title: ASMR Rotation Rate
sub_modules.content: High rotation speed around 60 rpm.

sub_modules.title: ASMR PRF
sub_modules.content: High PRF (4000 to 20000 pps) for detailed display.

sub_modules.title: ASMR Pulse Width
sub_modules.content: Very short pulse width (~0.03 µs) for high resolution.

sub_modules.title: ASMR Frequency
sub_modules.content: Operates in SHF band (15–17 GHz).

sub_modules.title: Weather Effects
sub_modules.content: High frequency signals are affected by precipitation.

sub_modules.title: Radar Resolution Requirement
sub_modules.content: High resolution needed to distinguish aircraft and vehicles on ground.

sub_modules.title: Radar Processing
sub_modules.content: Removes fixed objects to highlight moving targets.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: Ground radar systems provide surveillance and guidance at different ranges for safe ATC operations.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: AWR provides pilots with weather and terrain information ahead of the aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Purpose
sub_modules.content: Used for weather avoidance, turbulence detection and navigation.

sub_modules.title: Display
sub_modules.content: Shows range and bearing of targets relative to aircraft heading.

sub_modules.title: Colour Coding
sub_modules.content: Green (light), yellow (moderate), red (heavy), magenta (turbulence).

sub_modules.title: Components
sub_modules.content: Includes transmitter/receiver, antenna, indicator and control unit.

sub_modules.title: Antenna Stabilization
sub_modules.content: Antenna is stabilized in pitch and roll for accurate display.

sub_modules.title: Main Functions
sub_modules.content: Detect turbulence, measure cloud height, map terrain and provide navigation fixes.

sub_modules.title: Radar Type
sub_modules.content: AWR is a primary radar using the echo principle.

sub_modules.title: Echo Principle
sub_modules.content: Measures distance using reflected signals.

sub_modules.title: Searchlight Principle
sub_modules.content: Determines bearing using directional beam scanning.

sub_modules.title: Slant Range
sub_modules.content: Displayed range is slant distance, not ground distance.

sub_modules.title: Antenna Location
sub_modules.content: Mounted in aircraft nose for forward scanning.

sub_modules.title: Beam Types
sub_modules.content: Pencil beam for weather, fan beam for mapping.

sub_modules.title: Beamwidth
sub_modules.content: Typically 3° to 5° for weather detection.

sub_modules.title: Beam Tilt
sub_modules.content: Adjustable to scan above or below horizon.

sub_modules.title: Radar Frequency
sub_modules.content: Operates around 9375 MHz (≈3 cm wavelength).

sub_modules.title: Frequency Selection
sub_modules.content: Chosen to detect large water droplets associated with turbulence.

sub_modules.title: Radome Effects
sub_modules.content: Water or ice reduces radar performance.

sub_modules.title: Weather Detection
sub_modules.content: Detects precipitation intensity indicating turbulence.

sub_modules.title: Rainfall Gradient
sub_modules.content: Steeper gradient indicates higher turbulence risk.

sub_modules.title: Turbulence Indicators
sub_modules.content: Includes U-shapes, hooks, fingers and scalloped edges.

sub_modules.title: Control Unit
sub_modules.content: Allows adjustment of power, range, tilt and mode.

sub_modules.title: Range Selection
sub_modules.content: Typical ranges include 20, 50 and 150 NM.

sub_modules.title: Tilt Control
sub_modules.content: Adjusts vertical angle of radar beam.

sub_modules.title: Modes
sub_modules.content: Includes MAP, MANUAL, WEATHER and CONTOUR.

sub_modules.title: MAP Mode
sub_modules.content: Used for terrain mapping with fan-shaped beam.

sub_modules.title: MAN Mode
sub_modules.content: Manual gain control for mapping and weather.

sub_modules.title: WEA Mode
sub_modules.content: Automatic gain control for weather detection.

sub_modules.title: CONT Mode
sub_modules.content: Highlights intense precipitation and turbulence.

sub_modules.title: Automatic Gain Control
sub_modules.content: Maintains consistent display intensity regardless of range.

sub_modules.title: Mapping Operation
sub_modules.content: Uses downward tilt and fan beam for terrain depiction.

sub_modules.title: Ground Returns
sub_modules.content: Strong returns from cities, weak from water and sand.

sub_modules.title: Radar Shadow
sub_modules.content: Areas behind obstacles where radar cannot detect returns.

sub_modules.title: Pre-flight Safety
sub_modules.content: Radar should not be used near personnel due to radiation hazard.

sub_modules.title: Weather Operation
sub_modules.content: Used to avoid thunderstorms and severe turbulence.

sub_modules.title: Shadow Area Risk
sub_modules.content: Hidden weather may exist behind heavy rain.

sub_modules.title: Cloud Height Measurement
sub_modules.content: Estimated by adjusting tilt and using 1:60 rule.

sub_modules.title: Height Ring
sub_modules.content: Indicates aircraft height when no weather present.

sub_modules.title: Colour Radar Controls
sub_modules.content: Includes WX, WX+T, WX VAR and alert features.

sub_modules.title: Test Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays test pattern for system check.

sub_modules.title: Hold Function
sub_modules.content: Freezes display to track storm movement.

sub_modules.title: Target Alert
sub_modules.content: Warns of significant weather returns.

sub_modules.title: Fault Indication
sub_modules.content: Displays system failures.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: AWR helps pilots detect and avoid hazardous weather conditions.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: SSR is a radar system that uses aircraft transponder replies instead of reflected signals.

sub_modules.title: Primary vs Secondary Radar
sub_modules.content: Primary uses reflected echoes, SSR uses transmitted replies from aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Purpose
sub_modules.content: Provides identification, altitude and additional aircraft data to ATC.

sub_modules.title: Transponder
sub_modules.content: Aircraft equipment that responds to radar interrogation signals.

sub_modules.title: Code Setting
sub_modules.content: Pilot selects a 4-digit code assigned by ATC.

sub_modules.title: Transmission Type
sub_modules.content: Interrogation from ground, reply from aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Advantages of SSR
sub_modules.content: Lower power, clear display, aircraft identification, altitude and speed data.

sub_modules.title: SSR Range
sub_modules.content: Typically 200 to 250 NM.

sub_modules.title: SSR Display
sub_modules.content: Shows aircraft identity, altitude, speed, track and destination.

sub_modules.title: Operating Frequencies
sub_modules.content: Interrogation at 1030 MHz and reply at 1090 MHz.

sub_modules.title: Transmission Delay
sub_modules.content: Aircraft replies after 50 microseconds.

sub_modules.title: Radiation Pattern
sub_modules.content: Ground radar uses directional beam, aircraft transmits omni-directionally.

sub_modules.title: Modes
sub_modules.content: Mode A (identity) and Mode C (altitude).

sub_modules.title: Mode A
sub_modules.content: Provides aircraft identification code.

sub_modules.title: Mode C
sub_modules.content: Provides automatic altitude reporting referenced to 1013 hPa.

sub_modules.title: Pulse System
sub_modules.content: Uses P1, P2 and P3 pulses to determine interrogation mode.

sub_modules.title: Pulse Spacing
sub_modules.content: Mode A uses 8 µs spacing, Mode C uses 21 µs spacing.

sub_modules.title: Reply Structure
sub_modules.content: Contains framing pulses and 12 information pulses.

sub_modules.title: Code Capacity
sub_modules.content: 4096 possible codes (0000 to 7777).

sub_modules.title: SPI (Ident)
sub_modules.content: Special pulse to highlight aircraft on radar display.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Encoding
sub_modules.content: Altitude derived from encoder linked to altimeter.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Resolution
sub_modules.content: Reported in 100 ft steps (internally 50 ft increments).

sub_modules.title: SSR Procedures
sub_modules.content: Pilots follow ATC instructions for code selection and Mode C usage.

sub_modules.title: Special Codes
sub_modules.content: Emergency, radio failure and hijack codes.
General emergency.
Radio failure.
Hijack/unlawful interference.

sub_modules.title: Conspicuity Code
sub_modules.content: 7000 in Europe (1200 in USA) for VFR flights.

sub_modules.title: Garbling
sub_modules.content: Overlapping replies from multiple aircraft.

sub_modules.title: Fruiting
sub_modules.content: Interference from replies triggered by another radar.

sub_modules.title: Code Limitation
sub_modules.content: Limited number of available codes (4096).

sub_modules.title: Mode S
sub_modules.content: Advanced SSR with selective addressing and data link.

sub_modules.title: Mode S Address
sub_modules.content: Unique 24-bit aircraft address (~16 million codes).

sub_modules.title: Data Link
sub_modules.content: Allows air-ground and air-air communication.

sub_modules.title: Reduced Voice Communication
sub_modules.content: Data link reduces radio communication workload.

sub_modules.title: Improved Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Provides altitude in 25 ft increments.

sub_modules.title: Mode S Interrogations
sub_modules.content: Includes All Call, Broadcast and Selective interrogation.

sub_modules.title: Selective Addressing
sub_modules.content: Only targeted aircraft responds to interrogation.

sub_modules.title: Mode S Benefits
sub_modules.content: Improved tracking, reduced garbling, better identification.

sub_modules.title: Communication Protocols
sub_modules.content: Comm-A, Comm-B, Comm-C and Comm-D data exchanges.

sub_modules.title: Transponder Levels
sub_modules.content: Four levels defining capability from basic to full data link.

sub_modules.title: Level 1
sub_modules.content: Basic surveillance without data link.

sub_modules.title: Level 2
sub_modules.content: Standard data link, minimum for international use.

sub_modules.title: Level 3
sub_modules.content: Extended uplink communication.

sub_modules.title: Level 4
sub_modules.content: Full extended uplink and downlink capability.

sub_modules.title: DAPS
sub_modules.content: Downlink Aircraft Parameters such as speed, heading and vertical rate.

sub_modules.title: Future Capability
sub_modules.content: Includes transmission of aircraft intentions and selected flight parameters.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: SSR enhances radar by providing identification, altitude and data through transponder replies.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: DME is a secondary radar system that provides slant range distance from a ground station.

sub_modules.title: Rho-Theta Navigation
sub_modules.content: Combined with VOR provides range (rho) and bearing (theta).

sub_modules.title: Frequency Band
sub_modules.content: Operates in UHF band between 960 and 1215 MHz.

sub_modules.title: Channel System
sub_modules.content: 252 channels with 1 MHz spacing (X and Y channels).

sub_modules.title: Frequency Separation
sub_modules.content: 63 MHz difference between interrogation and reply frequencies.

sub_modules.title: VOR/DME Pairing
sub_modules.content: DME automatically selected when tuning VOR frequency.

sub_modules.title: ILS/DME Pairing
sub_modules.content: Provides distance to runway threshold.

sub_modules.title: Uses
sub_modules.content: Provides slant range, position fixing, groundspeed and approach guidance.

sub_modules.title: Slant Range
sub_modules.content: Measures direct distance between aircraft and station.

sub_modules.title: Position Fix
sub_modules.content: Two DMEs or VOR/DME provide position fix.

sub_modules.title: Groundspeed Calculation
sub_modules.content: Derived from rate of change of distance.

sub_modules.title: Pulse Technique
sub_modules.content: Uses pulse pairs for interrogation and reply.

sub_modules.title: Interrogation
sub_modules.content: Aircraft transmits pulses to ground station.

sub_modules.title: Reply
sub_modules.content: Ground station replies after 50 microseconds delay.

sub_modules.title: Range Measurement
sub_modules.content: Based on time delay of signal travel.

sub_modules.title: Pulse Pair Spacing
sub_modules.content: 12 µs for X channel and 36 µs for Y channel.

sub_modules.title: Random PRF
sub_modules.content: Aircraft transmits pulses at random intervals.

sub_modules.title: Lock-On
sub_modules.content: Receiver matches its own pulse pattern to track distance.

sub_modules.title: Tracking Mode
sub_modules.content: Continuously updates range after lock-on.

sub_modules.title: Range Search
sub_modules.content: Initial high-rate transmission to acquire signal.

sub_modules.title: Twin Pulses
sub_modules.content: Used to avoid interference and false signals.

sub_modules.title: Beacon Saturation
sub_modules.content: Occurs when too many aircraft use DME simultaneously.

sub_modules.title: Station Identification
sub_modules.content: 3-letter Morse code transmitted every 30 seconds.

sub_modules.title: Memory Circuit
sub_modules.content: Maintains display during signal interruption.

sub_modules.title: Line of Sight
sub_modules.content: Range depends on altitude and terrain.

sub_modules.title: Coverage
sub_modules.content: Limited by Designated Operational Coverage (DOC).

sub_modules.title: Echo Protection
sub_modules.content: Prevents errors from reflected signals.

sub_modules.title: System Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Approximately ±0.25 NM ±1.25% of range.

sub_modules.title: Precision DME
sub_modules.content: DME/P accuracy within ±100 ft.

sub_modules.title: Slant Range Error
sub_modules.content: Difference between slant and ground distance.

sub_modules.title: Cone of Confusion
sub_modules.content: Occurs when directly over station.

sub_modules.title: Height Indication
sub_modules.content: At zero range, DME indicates aircraft height.

sub_modules.title: Groundspeed Limitation
sub_modules.content: Accurate only when flying directly to or from station.

sub_modules.title: Circular Track Effect
sub_modules.content: Groundspeed shows zero when circling station.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: DME provides accurate slant range using pulse timing and is essential for navigation.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: RNAV allows aircraft to fly any desired path without flying directly over ground stations.

sub_modules.title: Definition
sub_modules.content: Navigation within coverage of navigation aids or self-contained systems.

sub_modules.title: Inputs
sub_modules.content: Uses VOR/DME, ILS/MLS, GNSS, INS/IRS, ADC and time.

sub_modules.title: System Processing
sub_modules.content: Combines inputs to compute accurate aircraft position.

sub_modules.title: Outputs
sub_modules.content: Provides course guidance, ETA and navigation information.

sub_modules.title: Benefits
sub_modules.content: Enables direct routing, reduces fuel, time and congestion.

sub_modules.title: Efficiency
sub_modules.content: Improves airspace utilization and route flexibility.

sub_modules.title: Separation Reduction
sub_modules.content: Allows reduced separation due to higher accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Types of RNAV
sub_modules.content: Includes Basic RNAV (B-RNAV) and Precision RNAV (P-RNAV).

sub_modules.title: B-RNAV
sub_modules.content: Accuracy within 5 NM for 95% of time.

sub_modules.title: P-RNAV
sub_modules.content: Accuracy within 1 NM for 95% of time.

sub_modules.title: Levels of RNAV
sub_modules.content: Includes 2D, 3D and 4D RNAV capabilities.

sub_modules.title: 2D RNAV
sub_modules.content: Horizontal navigation only.

sub_modules.title: 3D RNAV
sub_modules.content: Includes vertical navigation.

sub_modules.title: 4D RNAV
sub_modules.content: Includes time control along with position.

sub_modules.title: Simple RNAV Components
sub_modules.content: Includes navigation computer, CDU and CDI/HSI.

sub_modules.title: Waypoints
sub_modules.content: Defined positions used for navigation.

sub_modules.title: Phantom Stations
sub_modules.content: Waypoints treated like virtual navigation aids.

sub_modules.title: Rho-Theta Principle
sub_modules.content: Uses range and bearing from VOR/DME.

sub_modules.title: Cross Track Error
sub_modules.content: Difference between desired and actual track.

sub_modules.title: Distance to Go
sub_modules.content: Remaining distance to waypoint.

sub_modules.title: Waypoint Sequencing
sub_modules.content: Automatic switching between waypoints.

sub_modules.title: Limitations
sub_modules.content: Dependent on correct pilot input and beacon coverage.

sub_modules.title: DOC Requirement
sub_modules.content: Must remain within coverage of navigation aids.

sub_modules.title: Slant Range Error
sub_modules.content: DME error must be considered.

sub_modules.title: Accuracy Factors
sub_modules.content: Depends on geometry and position of navigation aids.

sub_modules.title: Modern RNAV
sub_modules.content: Implemented using Flight Management System (FMS).

sub_modules.title: FMS Components
sub_modules.content: Includes FMC, IRS, autopilot and displays.

sub_modules.title: FMC
sub_modules.content: Performs navigation and performance calculations.

sub_modules.title: CDU
sub_modules.content: Interface for pilot input and monitoring.

sub_modules.title: LNAV
sub_modules.content: Lateral navigation control.

sub_modules.title: VNAV
sub_modules.content: Vertical navigation control.

sub_modules.title: Flight Plan
sub_modules.content: Sequence of waypoints defining route.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Database
sub_modules.content: Contains routes, airports and navigation aids.

sub_modules.title: Performance Database
sub_modules.content: Contains aircraft performance data.

sub_modules.title: IRS Input
sub_modules.content: Provides position and heading information.

sub_modules.title: External Inputs
sub_modules.content: Includes DME/DME, VOR/DME and GNSS.

sub_modules.title: Kalman Filtering
sub_modules.content: Combines IRS and external data for accuracy.

sub_modules.title: IRS Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Accurate short-term but drifts over time.

sub_modules.title: DME Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Stable long-term accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Error Correction
sub_modules.content: FMS balances IRS drift with external references.

sub_modules.title: Automation Levels
sub_modules.content: Ranges from manual guidance to full autopilot control.

sub_modules.title: Route Programming
sub_modules.content: Waypoints entered via CDU.

sub_modules.title: Pre-flight Initialization
sub_modules.content: Includes position, route and database verification.

sub_modules.title: Climb, Cruise, Descent
sub_modules.content: FMS manages all flight phases.

sub_modules.title: Direct-To Function
sub_modules.content: Allows direct navigation to a waypoint.

sub_modules.title: Offset Tracks
sub_modules.content: Allows parallel routing.

sub_modules.title: Holding Patterns
sub_modules.content: RNAV can compute holding procedures.

sub_modules.title: Failure Indication
sub_modules.content: System alerts for navigation failures.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: RNAV enables efficient, flexible and accurate navigation using multiple inputs.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: EFIS provides electronic display of flight and navigation information.

sub_modules.title: EASA Standards
sub_modules.content: Defines colour coding and reliability requirements.

sub_modules.title: EHSI
sub_modules.content: Displays navigation, radar and TCAS information.

sub_modules.title: Inputs
sub_modules.content: Receives data from IRS, FMC, VOR, DME, ILS, ADF, TCAS and weather radar.

sub_modules.title: Symbol Generator
sub_modules.content: Processes inputs and generates display symbols.

sub_modules.title: EHSI Controller
sub_modules.content: Controls display modes, range and data selection.

sub_modules.title: Display Modes
sub_modules.content: Includes VOR, ILS, MAP and PLAN modes.

sub_modules.title: Full VOR Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays complete compass rose with VOR data.

sub_modules.title: Expanded VOR Mode
sub_modules.content: Shows enlarged forward view of VOR navigation.

sub_modules.title: Full ILS Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays ILS with full compass rose.

sub_modules.title: Expanded ILS Mode
sub_modules.content: Shows detailed ILS guidance.

sub_modules.title: MAP Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays route, waypoints and navigation data.

sub_modules.title: PLAN Mode
sub_modules.content: Displays route in true north orientation.

sub_modules.title: Orientation
sub_modules.content: Most modes are heading-based except PLAN mode.

sub_modules.title: Range Selection
sub_modules.content: Controls display distance scale.

sub_modules.title: Displayed Data
sub_modules.content: Includes nav aids, airports, routes, waypoints and weather.

sub_modules.title: TCAS Display
sub_modules.content: Shows traffic information when selected.

sub_modules.title: Weather Radar
sub_modules.content: Displays precipitation and turbulence.

sub_modules.title: Colour Coding
sub_modules.content: Standard ICAO/EASA colours used for clarity.

sub_modules.title: Warnings
sub_modules.content: Red indicates critical alerts.

sub_modules.title: Cautions
sub_modules.content: Amber indicates abnormal conditions.

sub_modules.title: Active Modes
sub_modules.content: Green indicates active systems.

sub_modules.title: Flight Guidance
sub_modules.content: Magenta indicates fly-to information.

sub_modules.title: Sky and Ground
sub_modules.content: Blue for sky and brown for earth.

sub_modules.title: Weather Colours
sub_modules.content: Green, amber, red and magenta indicate intensity.

sub_modules.title: Symbology
sub_modules.content: Standard symbols represent aircraft, route and navigation data.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Symbol
sub_modules.content: Represents aircraft position on display.

sub_modules.title: Waypoint Symbol
sub_modules.content: Shows navigation points.

sub_modules.title: Active Route
sub_modules.content: Displayed in magenta.

sub_modules.title: Inactive Route
sub_modules.content: Displayed in cyan.

sub_modules.title: Heading Information
sub_modules.content: Displays aircraft heading and selected heading.

sub_modules.title: Track Line
sub_modules.content: Shows actual ground track.

sub_modules.title: Wind Information
sub_modules.content: Displays wind speed and direction.

sub_modules.title: Trend Vector
sub_modules.content: Predicts aircraft future position.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Arc
sub_modules.content: Shows predicted altitude capture point.

sub_modules.title: Vertical Deviation
sub_modules.content: Indicates deviation from vertical path.

sub_modules.title: Glide Slope Indicator
sub_modules.content: Shows ILS glide slope deviation.

sub_modules.title: Lateral Deviation
sub_modules.content: Indicates course deviation.

sub_modules.title: Range Arcs
sub_modules.content: Shows distance intervals.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Aids
sub_modules.content: Displays VOR, DME, TACAN and ADF.

sub_modules.title: Airport Display
sub_modules.content: Shows airports and runways.

sub_modules.title: Fix Information
sub_modules.content: Displays selected reference points.

sub_modules.title: Holding Patterns
sub_modules.content: Displays holding procedures.

sub_modules.title: System Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Designed to avoid confusion and ensure reliability.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: EFIS integrates navigation data into a clear visual display for pilots.

sub_modules.title: Introduction
sub_modules.content: GNSS provides global satellite-based navigation with high accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Evolution
sub_modules.content: Developed from early satellite navigation systems to modern GNSS.

sub_modules.title: Systems
sub_modules.content: Includes GPS, GLONASS, Galileo and BeiDou.

sub_modules.title: Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Provides positioning accuracy in meters or better.

sub_modules.title: Differential Systems
sub_modules.content: Improve accuracy and integrity of GNSS.

sub_modules.title: Satellite Orbits
sub_modules.content: Satellites follow predictable Keplerian orbits.

sub_modules.title: Ephemeris
sub_modules.content: Satellite orbital position data.

sub_modules.title: Reference System
sub_modules.content: Uses Earth-centered Cartesian coordinates.

sub_modules.title: WGS84
sub_modules.content: Standard earth model used for GPS.

sub_modules.title: Segments
sub_modules.content: GNSS consists of space, control and user segments.

sub_modules.title: Space Segment
sub_modules.content: Satellites transmitting navigation signals.

sub_modules.title: Satellite Constellation
sub_modules.content: Multiple satellites ensure global coverage.

sub_modules.title: Satellite Clocks
sub_modules.content: Atomic clocks provide precise timing.

sub_modules.title: Frequencies
sub_modules.content: Signals transmitted on L1, L2 and other frequencies.

sub_modules.title: PRN Codes
sub_modules.content: Unique codes identify satellites and measure time.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Message
sub_modules.content: Contains satellite position, clock and system data.

sub_modules.title: Services
sub_modules.content: Includes standard positioning service (SPS) and precise positioning service (PPS).

sub_modules.title: Control Segment
sub_modules.content: Ground stations monitor and update satellites.

sub_modules.title: Monitoring Stations
sub_modules.content: Track satellite position and clock errors.

sub_modules.title: User Segment
sub_modules.content: Receivers determine position using satellite signals.

sub_modules.title: Receiver Types
sub_modules.content: Sequential, multiplex and multi-channel receivers.

sub_modules.title: Position Fixing
sub_modules.content: Uses pseudo-range measurements from satellites.

sub_modules.title: Pseudo-range
sub_modules.content: Initial range measurement with clock error.

sub_modules.title: Trilateration
sub_modules.content: Position determined by intersecting spheres.

sub_modules.title: Four Satellites
sub_modules.content: Required for 3D position and time correction.

sub_modules.title: Clock Error Correction
sub_modules.content: Receiver solves for position and time simultaneously.

sub_modules.title: 4D Fix
sub_modules.content: Provides position and accurate time.

sub_modules.title: Errors
sub_modules.content: Various factors affect GNSS accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Ephemeris Error
sub_modules.content: Satellite position inaccuracies.

sub_modules.title: Clock Error
sub_modules.content: Satellite clock inaccuracies.

sub_modules.title: Ionospheric Error
sub_modules.content: Signal delay in ionosphere.

sub_modules.title: Tropospheric Error
sub_modules.content: Signal delay due to atmospheric conditions.

sub_modules.title: Receiver Noise
sub_modules.content: Internal receiver errors.

sub_modules.title: Multipath
sub_modules.content: Signal reflections causing errors.

sub_modules.title: DOP
sub_modules.content: Dilution of precision due to satellite geometry.

sub_modules.title: HDOP
sub_modules.content: Horizontal accuracy degradation.

sub_modules.title: VDOP
sub_modules.content: Vertical accuracy degradation.

sub_modules.title: PDOP
sub_modules.content: Overall position accuracy.

sub_modules.title: TDOP
sub_modules.content: Time accuracy degradation.

sub_modules.title: Selective Availability
sub_modules.content: Intentional degradation removed in 2000.

sub_modules.title: System Accuracy
sub_modules.content: Typical accuracy is within meters.

sub_modules.title: Integrity
sub_modules.content: System must detect and warn errors quickly.

sub_modules.title: DGPS
sub_modules.content: Differential GPS improves accuracy.

sub_modules.title: ABAS
sub_modules.content: Aircraft-based integrity monitoring.

sub_modules.title: RAIM
sub_modules.content: Receiver autonomous integrity monitoring.

sub_modules.title: GBAS
sub_modules.content: Ground-based augmentation for precision approaches.

sub_modules.title: SBAS
sub_modules.content: Satellite-based augmentation systems.

sub_modules.title: EGNOS
sub_modules.content: European SBAS system.

sub_modules.title: WAAS
sub_modules.content: US SBAS system.

sub_modules.title: Geostationary Satellites
sub_modules.content: Used for augmentation signals.

sub_modules.title: Future Systems
sub_modules.content: Integration of multiple GNSS systems.

sub_modules.title: Summary
sub_modules.content: GNSS provides accurate global navigation using satellite signals.
